title: Telescope Array: Causal Wiring of Sensor Channels
isolation: candidate runs as an isolated stdin->stdout subprocess via isorun; the ground-truth DAG, topological order, edge weights and hidden state stay in the evaluator process
strategies: predict the empty graph (SHD = number of true edges = the evaluator baseline -> ~0.1 everywhere)
marginal-correlation thresholding with index-order orientation: catches real adjacencies but drowns in indirect-path false positives and coin-flip orientations

precision-matrix (partial-correlation) skeleton + equal-variance (ascending marginal variance) topological ordering to orient edges: removes indirect-path edges and recovers most orientations, but retains moralization edges between co-parents
constraint- or score-based discovery (PC-style conditional-independence tests, GES, or LiNGAM/equal-variance ordering search) that models each array's structure to push SHD lower still
